title: plugin-agent-opencode:plugin-agent
description: Open Code support is required to keep agent-plugin behavior consistent across the ecosystem and avoid a Claude-only installation path. This spec defines the Open Code plugin contract for skill install/uninstall, target install directory, and frontmatter handling so the plugin can be implemented predictably and validated against the same standards used by other agent plugins.
context: Open Code support is required to keep agent-plugin behavior consistent across the ecosystem and avoid a Claude-only installation path. This spec defines the Open Code plugin contract for skill install/uninstall, target install directory, and frontmatter handling so the plugin can be implemented predictably and validated against the same standards used by other agent plugins.

rules: The package MUST export `create(): AgentPlugin` as a named export.

The domain layer MUST define:

- a plugin runtime type implementing the `AgentPlugin` contract
- an Open Code frontmatter type reflecting the supported Open Code keys
- a per-skill frontmatter map keyed by skill name

The Open Code frontmatter model MUST support this exact field set:

- `name` (required)
- `description` (required)
- `license` (optional)
- `compatibility` (optional)
- `metadata` (optional `Record<string, string>`)

Unknown fields MUST NOT be emitted into generated `SKILL.md` files.

The application layer MUST include an `InstallSkills` use case that:

1. reads skills from `@specd/skills`
2. resolves the per-skill frontmatter map
3. prepends Open Code-compatible YAML frontmatter to markdown skill files
4. writes installed skills to the Open Code project skill directory
During install, the plugin MUST prepend YAML frontmatter to each markdown skill file and include only configured fields supported by Open Code.
Project-level skill installation MUST target `.opencode/skills/` under the provided `projectRoot`.
The interactive `specd project init` plugin selection MUST include `@specd/plugin-agent-opencode` in the known agent plugin options.
The `@specd/specd` meta package MUST declare `@specd/plugin-agent-opencode` as a workspace dependency.

`uninstall(projectRoot, options)` MUST remove installed skill directories from `.opencode/skills/`.

When `options.skills` is provided, uninstall MUST remove only the selected skill directories.

When `options.skills` is omitted, uninstall MUST remove the full `.opencode/skills/` tree.

constraints: The plugin MUST depend on @specd/skills for skill repository access.
The plugin MUST implement the AgentPlugin contract defined by @specd/plugin-manager.
The plugin MUST remain an adapter package with no domain logic outside plugin orchestration concerns.

scenarios.name: Exposes named create factory
then: a named create() export is available
AND calling create() returns an AgentPlugin

scenarios.name: Domain contract defines runtime and frontmatter types
then: a plugin runtime type implementing AgentPlugin is defined
AND an Open Code frontmatter type is defined with supported keys
AND a per-skill frontmatter map keyed by skill name is defined

scenarios.name: Frontmatter model enforces supported field set
when: frontmatter is generated for an installed SKILL.md
then: required fields name and description are present
AND optional fields license, compatibility, and metadata are emitted only when configured
AND unknown fields are not emitted

scenarios.name: InstallSkills follows required workflow
then: it reads skill templates, resolves per-skill frontmatter, prepends Open Code YAML frontmatter, and writes installed skill files

scenarios.name: Install prepends only Open Code-compatible fields
when: skill markdown files are written during install
then: YAML frontmatter is prepended before markdown content
AND emitted fields are limited to the configured Open Code-supported keys

scenarios.name: Skills install into Open Code directory
then: files are created under .opencode/skills/ within the provided projectRoot

scenarios.name: Wizard exposes Open Code plugin option
when: interactive specd project init renders plugin choices
then: @specd/plugin-agent-opencode appears in the known agent plugin options

scenarios.name: Meta package depends on Open Code plugin
then: @specd/plugin-agent-opencode is declared as a workspace:* dependency

scenarios.name: Uninstall removes selected skills when filter is provided
then: only the selected skill directories are removed

scenarios.name: Uninstall removes all skills when no filter is provided
